C:\Users\noura\source\repos\receipe app>gradlew.bat -p "C:\Users\noura\source\repos\receipe app\app"  assemblex86-64Debug
Starting a Gradle Daemon, 2 incompatible Daemons could not be reused, use --status for details
WARNING:We recommend using a newer Android Gradle plugin to use compileSdk = 33

This Android Gradle plugin (7.2.0) was tested up to compileSdk = 32

This warning can be suppressed by adding
    android.suppressUnsupportedCompileSdk=33
to this project's gradle.properties

The build will continue, but you are strongly encouraged to update your project to
use a newer Android Gradle Plugin that has been tested with compileSdk = 33
> Task :app:validateSigningX86-64Debug FAILED

FAILURE: Build failed with an exception.

* What went wrong:
Execution failed for task ':app:validateSigningX86-64Debug'.
> java.util.concurrent.ExecutionException: com.android.builder.utils.SynchronizedFile$ActionExecutionException: java.io.IOException: com.android.ide.common.signing.KeytoolException: Failed to create keystore.

* Try:
> Run with --stacktrace option to get the stack trace.
> Run with --info or --debug option to get more log output.
> Run with --scan to get full insights.

* Get more help at https://help.gradle.org

BUILD FAILED in 24s
?? actionable tasks: ? executed, ?? up-to-date

C:\Users\noura\source\repos\receipe app>

The build failed with an exception. It seems that there was an issue with validating the signing configuration for the x86-64Debug variant of the app. The specific error message states that there was a problem creating the keystore.

To get more information about the error and find a solution, you can try the following:

1. Run the build command again with the `--stacktrace` option to get the full stack trace, which might provide more details about the error:

   ```
   gradlew.bat -p "C:\Users\noura\source\repos\receipe app\app" assemblex86-64Debug --stacktrace
   ```

2. If the problem is related to the keystore, make sure that the keystore file exists and is accessible. Double-check the keystore file paths and permissions.

3. Verify that the keystore password and other signing configurations (such as key alias and key password) are correct. Make sure they match the values specified in your project's Gradle files.

4. If you recently updated any tools or libraries, ensure that they are compatible with the Android Gradle plugin version you are using. Consider updating the Android Gradle plugin and other dependencies to their latest versions to ensure compatibility.

5. Check if there are any additional error messages or warnings in the build logs that could provide more context about the issue.

If none of the above solutions solve the problem, consider seeking help from the Android Gradle plugin documentation or the Gradle community to get further assistance.
how to add keystore to gradle build

C:\Users\noura\source\repos\receipe app>

The build failed with an exception indicating that there is a syntax error in the `build.gradle` file located at `C:\Users\noura\source\repos\receipe app\app`.

Based on the error message, the problem seems to be at line 3, column 9, specifically at the opening curly brace `{` of the `android` block.

To resolve this issue, make sure that your `build.gradle` file is properly formatted and doesn't contain any syntax errors. Double-check the content of your `build.gradle` file and ensure that the `android` block is correctly defined.

If you are unsure about the correct syntax or structure, consider providing the content of your `build.gradle` file so that I can help you identify any potential errors.
